title: Publications | Ministry of Health NZ
content:
## Publications Ngā whakaputanga kōrero
- Government Response to the Report from the Prime Minister’s Chief Science Advisor Publication date: 20 February 2025 This publication responds to and notes progress made against recommendations in the report from the Office of the Prime Minister’s Chief Science Advisor Kotahitanga : Uniting Aotearoa against infectious disease and antimicrobial resistance published in March 2022. Publication Antimicrobial resistance Public health

## Government Response to the Report from the Prime Minister’s Chief Science Advisor
This publication responds to and notes progress made against recommendations in the report from the Office of the Prime Minister’s Chief Science Advisor Kotahitanga : Uniting Aotearoa against infectious disease and antimicrobial resistance published in March 2022.
- Antimicrobial resistance
- The Risk of Adverse Events Associated with Mesh and Non-Mesh Repair of Groin Hernias: A literature review Publication date: 13 February 2025 This paper reviews the current literature on the risk of adverse events associated with the use of mesh in groin hernia repair. Publication Surgical mesh

## The Risk of Adverse Events Associated with Mesh and Non-Mesh Repair of Groin Hernias: A literature review
This paper reviews the current literature on the risk of adverse events associated with the use of mesh in groin hernia repair.
- Office of the Director of Mental Health and Addiction Services: Regulatory Report 1 July 2022 to 30 June 2023 Publication date: 13 February 2025 The Office of the Director of Mental Health and Addiction Services Regulatory Report 1 July 2022 to 30 June 2023 provides information and statistics relating to the use of compulsory mental health assessment and treatment legislation in Aotearoa New Zealand. Publication Mental health

## Office of the Director of Mental Health and Addiction Services: Regulatory Report 1 July 2022 to 30 June 2023
The Office of the Director of Mental Health and Addiction Services Regulatory Report 1 July 2022 to 30 June 2023 provides information and statistics relating to the use of compulsory mental health assessment and treatment legislation in Aotearoa New Zealand.
- Public Health Surveillance Strategy 2025–2030 Publication date: 5 February 2025 The Public Health Surveillance Strategy sets the direction for strengthening the public health surveillance system over the next five years. Publication Diseases & conditions Health system Public health

## Public Health Surveillance Strategy 2025–2030
The Public Health Surveillance Strategy sets the direction for strengthening the public health surveillance system over the next five years.
- Diseases & conditions
- Prolonged Symptoms Attributable to Infection with COVID-19 Publication date: 27 January 2025 This evidence brief synthesizes the international literature on prolonged symptoms that can be attributed to infection with COVID-19 (long COVID). Publication COVID-19

## Prolonged Symptoms Attributable to Infection with COVID-19
This evidence brief synthesizes the international literature on prolonged symptoms that can be attributed to infection with COVID-19 (long COVID).
- Tatau Kahukura : Māori Health Chart Book 2024 Publication date: 19 December 2024 Tatau Kahukura 2024 provides a snapshot of the health of Māori compared with non-Māori. It presents key indicators relating to the socioeconomic determinants of health, risk and protective factors for health, health status, health service use and the health system. Tatau Kahukura shows that Māori have higher rates than non-Māori for many health conditions and chronic diseases, including cancer, diabetes, cardiovascular disease and asthma. Publication Māori health Statistics

## Tatau Kahukura : Māori Health Chart Book 2024
Tatau Kahukura 2024 provides a snapshot of the health of Māori compared with non-Māori. It presents key indicators relating to the socioeconomic determinants of health, risk and protective factors for health, health status, health service use and the health system. Tatau Kahukura shows that Māori have higher rates than non-Māori for many health conditions and chronic diseases, including cancer, diabetes, cardiovascular disease and asthma.
- Strategy to Prevent and Minimise Gambling Harm 2025/26 to 2027/28: Proposals document Publication date: 19 December 2024 The Ministry has reviewed the submissions received to its draft strategy consultation document and prepared this proposals document for the Gambling Commission to consult on as the next step in developing the refreshed strategy and levy rates. Publication Gambling harm

## Strategy to Prevent and Minimise Gambling Harm 2025/26 to 2027/28: Proposals document
The Ministry has reviewed the submissions received to its draft strategy consultation document and prepared this proposals document for the Gambling Commission to consult on as the next step in developing the refreshed strategy and levy rates.
- Interim Government Policy Statement Measures Snapshot Report 2023/24 Publication date: 17 December 2024 This snapshot report presents national results of measures from the interim Government Policy Statement for the second and final year (2023/24) of the two-year interim period (2022-2024). Publication Health system

## Interim Government Policy Statement Measures Snapshot Report 2023/24
This snapshot report presents national results of measures from the interim Government Policy Statement for the second and final year (2023/24) of the two-year interim period (2022-2024).
- Abortion Services Aotearoa New Zealand: Annual Report 2024 Publication date: 17 December 2024 The Abortion Services Aotearoa New Zealand Annual Report 2024 provides a snapshot of abortion services for the 2023 calendar year. Publication Abortion Statistics Sexual & reproductive health

## Abortion Services Aotearoa New Zealand: Annual Report 2024
The Abortion Services Aotearoa New Zealand Annual Report 2024 provides a snapshot of abortion services for the 2023 calendar year.
- Sexual & reproductive health
- Centres of Excellence in Health and Disability Service Delivery: Evidence brief Publication date: 13 December 2024 This Evidence Brief provides insight into the impact of ‘Centres of Excellence’ for specialised health or disabling conditions and was used to inform the Rare Disorders Strategy published in February 2024. Publication Disability support

## Centres of Excellence in Health and Disability Service Delivery: Evidence brief
This Evidence Brief provides insight into the impact of ‘Centres of Excellence’ for specialised health or disabling conditions and was used to inform the Rare Disorders Strategy published in February 2024.
- Health Care and Rare Disorders: Evidence brief Publication date: 13 December 2024 This Evidence Brief is a review of national and international evidence and was used to inform the Rare Disorders Strategy published in February 2024. Publication Diseases & conditions

## Health Care and Rare Disorders: Evidence brief
This Evidence Brief is a review of national and international evidence and was used to inform the Rare Disorders Strategy published in February 2024.
- Diseases & conditions
- Community Water Fluoridation: An evidence review Publication date: 4 December 2024 This review updates the evidence regarding community water fluoridation published since the 2021 report from the Office of the Prime Minister’s Chief Science Advisor. Publication Drinking-water Oral health Public health

## Community Water Fluoridation: An evidence review
This review updates the evidence regarding community water fluoridation published since the 2021 report from the Office of the Prime Minister’s Chief Science Advisor.
- Code of Practice for Diagnostic and Interventional Radiology: ORS C1 Publication date: 1 December 2024 This code applies to all activities associated with radiological equipment used for diagnostic radiology, interventional radiology, the dental use of cone beam computed tomography equipment and research. Publication Radiation safety

## Code of Practice for Diagnostic and Interventional Radiology: ORS C1
This code applies to all activities associated with radiological equipment used for diagnostic radiology, interventional radiology, the dental use of cone beam computed tomography equipment and research.
- Code of Practice for Dental Radiology: ORS C4 Publication date: 1 December 2024 The Code of Practice for Dental Radiology applies to all activities associated with radiological equipment used for intra-oral, panoramic and cephalometric dental procedures. It provides details necessary to comply with the Radiation Safety Act 2016. Publication Radiation safety

## Code of Practice for Dental Radiology: ORS C4
The Code of Practice for Dental Radiology applies to all activities associated with radiological equipment used for intra-oral, panoramic and cephalometric dental procedures. It provides details necessary to comply with the Radiation Safety Act 2016.
- Clearing the Air: Assessing real-world ventilation practices in New Zealand Publication date: 29 November 2024 The Ministry of Health commissioned the National Institute of Water and Atmospheric Research (NIWA) to conduct research on ventilation in indoor settings and trial a range of possible interventions. Publication Environmental health Public health

## Clearing the Air: Assessing real-world ventilation practices in New Zealand
The Ministry of Health commissioned the National Institute of Water and Atmospheric Research (NIWA) to conduct research on ventilation in indoor settings and trial a range of possible interventions.
